response.justification: The candidate is a full-stack web and TypeScript developer whose career has virtually no C++ experience — the role's hard minimum of 8 years is unmet by a wide margin; the only C++ listed is a brief ~6-month stint at National Instruments and a university thesis. Large-scale cloud compute platform ownership, which is the core of this role, does not appear anywhere in the career history. While the candidate demonstrates strong software-craftsmanship values, genuine distributed-systems exposure at Sociomantic, and a breadth of runtimes, these do not compensate for the fundamental mismatch in language expertise and infrastructure domain focus.
strengths: Hands-on exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second), demonstrating some performance-sensitive infrastructure awareness
Consistent software-craftsmanship practices — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development — and active knowledge-sharing through community workshops and conference talks
Breadth across runtimes and cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Node.js, D) suggests adaptability and willingness to operate outside comfort zones
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments plus a thesis project), critically short of the 8-year hard minimum required by the role
No cloud compute platform ownership or large-scale infrastructure architecture; AWS CDK usage at Optilyz is scoped to a pricing-team context, not platform-level work

No formal technical leadership of cloud or DevOps engineers; staff-level contributions and workshop facilitation exist but are not equivalent to the strategic mentoring and cross-team alignment this role demands

key_factors.factor: Absence of cloud compute platform ownership
